summary.en: As Bogotá’s historic district, La Candelaria is the bohemian heart of the city, defined by narrow cobblestone streets, vibrant street art, and striking colonial architecture.
A hectic and authentic balance between old and new, where century-old plazas and traditional markets sit in the shadow of towering commercial skyscrapers.
A bohemian enclave of artsy cafes and galleries sharply contrasting with the adjacent 1950s financial center of wide avenues and massive glass skyscrapers.
An incredibly diverse and elegant district that mixes the LGBTQ+-friendly 'Chapigay' area with the pristine 1930s English architecture of Quinta Camacho.
The most chic, upscale, and cosmopolitan area of Bogotá, featuring high-end boutiques and Michelin-starred dining alongside lush, peaceful green spaces.
Originally a separate colonial town, Usaquén retains the feel of a 'little pueblo in the big city' with cobblestone streets shadowed by ultra-modern financial centers.

description.en: A deeply charming, multi-level bohemian spot near the city's birthplace, offering hidden nooks, a cozy atmosphere, and a rooftop with fireplaces.
Located inside a colonial house, this lively spot serves up budget drinks and a very sociable, international backpacker atmosphere.
A top-tier farm-to-table restaurant inside a breathtaking historic house renovated with bamboo and recycled pipes by architect Simon Velez.
A small, friendly, and highly-rated independent eatery serving up excellent Middle-Eastern and Thai dishes like shawarmas and kebabs.
The legendary, unofficial founding site of Bogotá from 1538, now a bohemian gathering place for arts, culture, and music.
A tiny, hidden-gem gallery dedicated to the local street art scene, where you can buy original works directly from the artists who paint the neighborhood.
An often-overlooked free museum detailing the history of Colombian moneymaking and vintage coins.
A quiet, local favorite offering very good, reasonably priced breakfasts and fresh fruit juices.
A friendly social economy cooperative that functions as an affordable local cafe and supermarket, ideal for a fresh breakfast or meal of the day.
An excellent, creative eatery offering high-quality daily meals and fresh fruit juices at accessible local prices.
A bustling fast-food joint famous for serving a massive 'Godzilla' empanada—likely the biggest on the continent.
A completely hidden, narrow hall filled with small, picturesque stores selling inexpensive, authentic Colombian handicrafts.
A beautifully designed tree-lined pedestrian axis that features an enclosed water stream running right through the concrete downtown.
A vibrant, chaotic local plaza surrounded by cheap stores selling absolutely everything from clothing to live pets.
A sky-high club and lounge on the 41st floor of a skyscraper, offering big techno DJs and unparalleled views of the sprawling city.
A tiny, atmospheric tapas bar boasting a fantastic drink selection and live Flamenco music on Thursday nights.
An unmarked, tiny one-room restaurant with an open kitchen and tons of local atmosphere, specializing in Spanish-inspired tapas.
An iconic, modernist residential skyscraper complex designed by the legendary Colombian architect Rogelio Salmona in 1968.
A completely unexpected, quiet enclave hidden amidst skyscrapers, featuring Georgian and Tudor-style mansions that look like they were plucked from London.
A newly renovated dome cinema that features Friday night telescope observations and a beautiful roof terrace.
A highly charming, vibe-heavy spot with a great drink menu, an amazing atmosphere, and a live band on weekends.
One of the largest and most famous nightclubs in the city, holding up to 6,000 people and serving as the epicenter of Bogotá’s LGBTQ+ nightlife.
A historic institution defending authentic Creole cuisine in Chapinero since 1934, famous for traditional dishes like creole flank steak.
A former bakery transformed into a Parisian-style cafe, renowned among locals for its pan de chocolate.
A 1930s planned suburb where historic English-style mansions have been converted into indie shops and cafes.
A heavily guarded, secret nature path leading up into the eastern hills behind Bogotá, offering serene hiking and spectacular views.

name.en: Basilica of Our Lady of Lourdes

description.en: A massive, striking Neo-gothic church that anchors the older Chapinero Centro neighborhood.
A stylish local favorite in the Zona T known for serving the best martinis in town, specifically their signature Lychee Martini.
An incredibly cool, high-energy spot near Parque 93 where locals gather for beers, great food, and spontaneous salsa dancing.
A timeless, intimate, and romantic restaurant that has been a local icon since 1996, featuring a gorgeous open terrace on Parque 93.
A vibrant, traditional Mexican eatery beloved by locals for its excellent margaritas and lively atmosphere.
A long, beautiful, tree-lined park featuring a running creek, offering a literal breath of fresh air amidst the dense urban jungle.
An old 18th-century hacienda surrounded by artificial creeks and botanical gardens, housing a quirky museum of global porcelain.
A completely walkable, T-shaped intersection packed with luxury fashion, outdoor cafes, and the city's elite.
An elegant, hidden-away spot located down a side street off the main park, featuring excellent drinks and live Jazz on Thursdays.
Bogotá’s original craft brewery. Order the 'Jirafa' (a yard-long glass of beer) and enjoy the lively, authentic local crowd.
A highly innovative Spanish/Catalonian fusion restaurant focusing on molecular cooking and eclectic local ingredients.
A cozy, rustic spot located just north of the main square, serving outstanding, elevated Colombian home-made cuisine.
An expansive, vibrant outdoor market that takes over the colonial thoroughfares every Sunday, filled with local artisans and street performers.
A fascinating architectural oddity where a 19th-century mansion was seamlessly converted into an upscale mall with hidden cafes.
The heart of the historic district, lined with trees, old colonial facades, and 400-year-old churches.

prompt.en: Capture a rooftop view facing downtown Bogotá at sunset from the terrace of Gato Gris.
Find and photograph one of the charming, hidden nooks inside the multi-level Gato Gris.
Take a photo that captures the sociable, international backpacker atmosphere of the bar.
Capture an image of the bar that highlights its location inside a historic colonial house.
Photograph a dish from this farm-to-table restaurant, with the unique bamboo and recycled pipe architecture in the background.
Take a picture that showcases the breathtaking interior of the renovated historic house.
Get a close-up shot of an excellent shawarma or kebab from this highly-rated eatery.
Capture the small and friendly storefront of this independent restaurant.
Take a photo that captures the arts, culture, and music of the crowd gathered in the plaza.
Locate and photograph one of the eccentric, historic street names painted on picturesque signs at the corners around the plaza.
Photograph the tiny, hidden-gem entrance to the DIBS gallery.
Find a piece of original work inside the gallery and take a photo that captures its essence.
Take a photo of the vintage Colombian coin collection inside the museum.
Capture the exterior of this free museum, often overlooked by tourists.
Photograph a delicious and reasonably priced breakfast at this local favorite.
Capture the vibrant color of one of their fresh fruit juices.
Take a photo that shows the friendly, social economy vibe of this cooperative cafe.
Capture a shot of the affordable and fresh 'meal of the day' offered here.
Photograph one of the high-quality, creative daily meals served at this eatery.
Take a picture that conveys the high quality of the food in its accessible, local setting.
Take a photo of the massive 'Godzilla' empanada next to a standard object for scale.
Capture the bustling atmosphere of this fast-food joint catering to university students.
Photograph the narrow, completely hidden hall filled with picturesque stores.
Find a unique, authentic Colombian handicraft and take a detailed photo of it.
Photograph the flowing water stream of the Eje Ambiental contrasting with the towering 19th-century buildings.
Capture the beautifully designed, tree-lined pedestrian path that runs through downtown.
Take a photo that captures the vibrant and chaotic energy of this local plaza.
Find the most unusual item for sale in the cheap stores surrounding the plaza and snap a picture.
Get a breathtaking nighttime shot looking out over the city lights from the 41st-floor windows of Centrico.
Capture the atmosphere of the club, showing the DJ and the crowd with the city view in the background.
If it's a Thursday, capture the energy of the live Flamenco music performance.
Take a photo that shows off the tiny, atmospheric interior of this popular tapas bar.
Photograph the unmarked, one-room exterior of this local favorite restaurant.
Get a shot of the open kitchen, capturing the local atmosphere and the chefs at work.
Capture the unique, iconic modernist design of this residential complex by Rogelio Salmona.
Take a photo showing how the iconic towers integrate with the surrounding park and cityscape.
Take a picture of the London-style Tudor mansions in La Merced with a towering glass skyscraper looming in the background.
Get a close-up shot of the architectural details on one of the Georgian or Tudor-style mansions.
Capture the unique architecture of the newly renovated dome cinema.
Take a photo from the beautiful roof terrace of the planetarium.
Capture a photo that shows off the charming, vibe-heavy atmosphere of this spot.
If it's a weekend, take a picture of the live band performing.
Take a photo that conveys the massive scale of one of the largest nightclubs in the city.
Capture an image that represents the alternative and LGBTQ+ friendly atmosphere of Theatron.
Photograph the famous traditional dish, 'sobrebarriga a la criolla' (creole flank steak).
Take a photo of the exterior of this historic institution that has been defending Creole cuisine for decades.
Capture the Parisian-style ambiance of this transformed bakery.
Take a delicious-looking close-up of the 'pan de chocolate' that it's renowned for.
Find and photograph one of the specific Parisian or English-style converted mansions hidden in the Quinta Camacho sector.
Take a photo showing one of the historic mansions repurposed as a modern indie shop or cafe.
Hike Quebrada La Vieja right at 7:00 AM to photograph the sweeping views of Bogotá’s skyline nestled against the mountains.
Capture an image of the serene, heavily guarded nature path leading up into the hills.
Take a photo that captures the massive and striking Neo-gothic architecture of the basilica.
Get a shot that shows how the church anchors the older Chapinero Centro neighborhood.
Order and photograph the signature Lychee Martini, known as the best in town.
Capture the stylish atmosphere that makes this a favorite among locals in the Zona T.
Capture a photo of the spontaneous salsa dancing that breaks out between the tables on weekends.
Take a picture that shows the incredibly cool, high-energy vibe of the local crowd.
Take a photo of the gorgeous open terrace that captures the intimate and romantic atmosphere.
Get a shot of the restaurant showing its prime location right on Parque 93.
Photograph one of the excellent margaritas this eatery is beloved for.
Capture the vibrant, traditional Mexican decor and lively atmosphere.
Capture a photo of the creek running specifically through the center of Parque del Virrey.
Take a picture of the long, tree-lined park, showing how it offers a green escape in the urban jungle.
Photograph the peaceful artificial creeks winding through the historic gardens of Museo El Chico.
Capture the architecture of the old 18th-century hacienda that houses the museum.
Take a photo of the completely walkable, T-shaped intersection, capturing the luxury fashion and elite crowd.
Find a bustling outdoor cafe in the Zona T and capture the chic, cosmopolitan scene.
If it's a Thursday night, capture a photo of the live Jazz performance.
Take a picture that shows the elegant, hidden-away nature of this spot on a side street.
Order and photograph the 'Jirafa,' a yard-long glass of beer, unique to this brewery.
Capture the lively and authentic local crowd enjoying Bogotá's original craft beer.
Take an artistic photo of a dish that showcases the restaurant's focus on molecular cooking.
Capture an image that represents the innovative Spanish/Catalonian fusion concept of the restaurant.
Photograph a plate of the outstanding, elevated Colombian home-made cuisine.
Take a photo that captures the cozy, rustic atmosphere of this spot just north of the main square.
Find and photograph a live ballet performer or storyteller working the crowds during the Sunday flea market.
Capture a close-up of a unique item being sold by a local artisan at the market.
Snap a picture capturing the 19th-century colonial facade of Hacienda Santa Barbara with a modern glass skyscraper looming directly behind it.
Take a photo from inside the mall that shows how the historic 19th-century mansion has been integrated into the modern shopping center.
Photograph the old colonial facades that line the heart of Usaquén's historic district.
Capture an image of one of the 400-year-old churches on the main square.
